title_en: Influence and Place in Athari Literature
title_id: Pengaruh dan Kedudukan dalam Literatur Athari
content_id:
Syarh Usul I'tiqad Ahlus Sunnah menempati kedudukan penting dalam kanon literatur akidah Athari bersama karya-karya seperti al-Ibanah karya al-Asy'ari (dalam bentuk akidah pra-Asy'ari), As-Sunnah karya Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal, al-Ibanah karya Ibn Baththah, dan koleksi-koleksi Usul as-Sunnah yang dinisbatkan kepada berbagai ulama awal. Keistimewaannya dalam genre ini adalah cakupannya yang komprehensif dan kecermatan al-Lalika'i dalam mengumpulkan dan mengorganisasi materinya.

Dalam tradisi ilmiah Hanbali dan Athari yang lebih luas, karya ini diakui sebagai rujukan yang otoritatif. Ibn Taimiyyah mengutipnya dengan sering. Ibn al-Qayyim menimba darinya. Para ulama Hanbali kemudian di era Utsmani terus merujuknya. Edisi kritis modern, yang diterbitkan oleh Dar Thaybah di Riyadh pada abad kedua puluh, membawa karya tersebut ke khalayak yang jauh lebih luas dan membuatnya dapat diakses oleh para pelajar di seluruh dunia Islam.

Pengaruh karya ini melampaui tradisi Athari secara khusus. Para ulama yang tertarik pada sejarah akidah Islam secara keseluruhan — termasuk mereka yang bekerja dalam kerangka Asy'ari dan Maturidi — telah menemukan nilainya sebagai sumber dokumenter. Karena al-Lalika'i melestarikan pernyataan-pernyataan dari ratusan ulama awal, banyak di antaranya karya-karya independennya tidak tersisa, Syarh Usul I'tiqad berfungsi sebagai sumber primer untuk sejarah teologi Islam dalam periode formatifnya.

Bagi pelajar-pelajar teologi Islam kontemporer, karya ini mengajukan pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang produktif tentang hubungan antara pendekatan-pendekatan yang ditransmisikan dan yang rasional dalam memahami akidah. Tradisi Athari, sebagaimana diwakili oleh al-Lalika'i, menegaskan bahwa konsensus komunitas awal tentang perkara-perkara akidah memiliki otoritas yang mengikat yang tidak dapat dikesampingkan oleh inovasi-inovasi filosofis. Tradisi-tradisi Asy'ari dan Maturidi, meskipun berbagi banyak posisi substantif dengan mazhab Athari tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan spesifik, percaya bahwa alat-alat kalam diperlukan untuk membela keimanan dari tantangan filosofis dengan cara-cara yang pendekatan-pendekatan yang murni ditransmisikan tidak dapat melakukannya.

Ini bukan sekadar perdebatan historis. Dalam pendidikan dan wacana Islam kontemporer, pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang peran yang tepat dari teologi rasional dalam memahami dan membela akidah Islam tetap hidup. Syarh Usul I'tiqad mewakili kutub tradisionalis dari percakapan yang sedang berlangsung ini dengan dokumentasi dan keluasan yang luar biasa. Ia mendemonstrasikan, lebih lengkap dari hampir semua teks tunggal lainnya, seperti apa konsensus akidah komunitas Muslim awal sebagaimana dicatat oleh para kontemporer dan orang-orang yang dekat dengan zamannya.

Al-Lalika'i wafat pada 418 H di Baghdad. Karyanya bertahan sebagai monumen dari tradisi ilmiah dokumentasi akidah berbasis hadis dan terus dibaca, diajarkan, dan dikutip di seluruh dunia Islam sebagai salah satu rujukan mendasar untuk memahami keyakinan-keyakinan Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah.
content_en:
Sharh Usul I'tiqad Ahl us-Sunnah holds an important place in the canon of Athari creedal literature alongside works like al-Ibanah of al-Ash'ari (in its pre-Ash'ari creedal form), As-Sunnah of Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Al-Ibana of Ibn Battah, and the Usul as-Sunnah collections attributed to various early scholars. Its distinction within this genre is its comprehensive scope and the care with which al-Lalika'i assembled and organized his material.

Within the Hanbali and broader Athari scholarly tradition, the work was recognized as an authoritative reference. Ibn Taymiyyah cited it frequently. Ibn al-Qayyim drew on it. Later Hanbali scholars of the Ottoman period continued to reference it. The modern critical edition, published by Dar Taybah in Riyadh in the twentieth century, brought the work to a much wider audience and made it accessible to students across the Islamic world.

The work's influence extended beyond the specifically Athari tradition. Scholars interested in the history of Islamic creed as a whole — including those working within Ash'ari and Maturidi frameworks — have found it valuable as a documentary source. Because al-Lalika'i preserves statements from hundreds of early scholars, many of whose independent works are not extant, Sharh Usul I'tiqad serves as a primary source for the history of Islamic theology in its formative period.

For contemporary students of Islamic theology, the work raises productive questions about the relationship between transmitted and rational approaches to creed. The Athari tradition, as represented by al-Lalika'i, insists that the early community's consensus on creedal matters has a binding authority that philosophical innovations cannot override. The Ash'ari and Maturidi traditions, while sharing many substantive positions with the Athari school on specific questions, believe that kalam tools are necessary to defend the faith against philosophical challenge in ways that purely transmitted approaches cannot do.

These are not merely historical debates. In contemporary Islamic education and discourse, questions about the proper role of rational theology in understanding and defending Islamic creed remain live. Sharh Usul I'tiqad represents the traditionalist pole of this ongoing conversation with exceptional documentation and breadth. It demonstrates, more completely than almost any other single text, what the creedal consensus of the early Muslim community looked like as recorded by contemporaries and near-contemporaries.

Al-Lalika'i died in 418 AH in Baghdad. His work survived as a monument to the scholarly tradition of hadith-based creedal documentation and continues to be read, taught, and cited across the Islamic world as one of the foundational references for understanding the beliefs of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah.
content_ar: Sharh الأصول I'tiqad Ahl us-السنة holds an المهم place in the canon of الأثري creedal literature alongside works like al-Ibanah of al-الأشعري (in its pre-الأشعري creedal form), As-السنة of Abdullah ibn أحمد ابن حنبل, Al-Ibana of Ibn Battah, and the الأصول as-السنة collections attributed to various early العلماء. Its distinction within this genre is its comprehensive scope and the care with which al-Lalika'i assembled and organized his material. Within the الحنبلي and broader الأثري العلمي التراث, the work was recognized as an المعتمد reference. ابن تيمية cited it frequently. ابن القيم drew on it. Later الحنبلي العلماء of the Ottoman period continued to reference it. The modern البالغ الأهمية edition, published by Dar Taybah in Riyadh in the twentieth century, brought the work to a much wider audience and made it accessible to students across the Islamic الدنيا. The work's influence extended beyond the specifically الأثري التراث. العلماء interested in the history of Islamic creed as a whole — بما في ذلك those working within الأشعري and الماتريدي frameworks — have found it valuable as a documentary source. لأن al-Lalika'i preserves statements from hundreds of early العلماء, many of whose independent works are not extant, Sharh الأصول I'tiqad serves as a الأساسي source for the history of Islamic theology in its formative period. For contemporary students of Islamic theology, the work raises productive questions about the relationship between transmitted and rational approaches to creed. The الأثري التراث, as represented by al-Lalika'i, insists that the early الجماعة's consensus on creedal matters has a binding authority that philosophical innovations cannot override. The الأشعري and الماتريدي traditions, بينما sharing many substantive positions with the الأثري school on specific questions, believe that علم الكلام tools are necessary to defend the الإيمان against philosophical challenge in ways that purely transmitted approaches cannot do. These are not merely historical debates. In contemporary Islamic education and discourse, questions about the proper role of rational theology in الفهم and defending Islamic creed remain live. Sharh الأصول I'tiqad represents the traditionalist pole of this ongoing conversation with exceptional documentation and breadth. It demonstrates, more completely than almost any other single text, what the creedal consensus of the early مسلم الجماعة looked like as recorded by contemporaries and near-contemporaries. Al-Lalika'i died in 418 AH in Baghdad. His work survived as a monument to the العلمي التراث of الحديث-based creedal documentation and continues to be read, taught, and cited across the Islamic الدنيا as one of the foundational references for الفهم the beliefs of Ahl us-السنة wal-Jama'ah.
